title: Share of population with large expenditures on health
subtitle: The share of the population that spend more than 10% of total household expenditure or income on health.
caption:
Share of population with large expenditures on health

The share of the population that spend more than 10% of total household expenditure or income on health.

This choropleth world map displays the share of the population with large health expenditures in 2021, defined as spending more than 10% of total household expenditure or income on health. The legend ranges from 0% to 35%, with color bands marking 5% intervals. The darkest red shades, indicating values above 30%, are visible in Angola and Egypt. China and several countries in Central Asia and the Middle East, including Iran and Iraq, appear in the orange bands corresponding to the 20% to 30% range. A broad cluster of nations across South America, including Brazil and Argentina, along with parts of Eastern Europe and Southeast Asia, sit in the mid-range yellow-orange shades, generally falling between 10% and 20%. North America, Western Europe, and Australia are predominantly colored in the lightest yellow shades, representing values below 10%. Large areas of the map, including Greenland, much of Central Africa, and several island nations, feature a diagonal hatching pattern, indicating that no data is available for these regions. The visual distribution highlights higher shares of the population facing large health expenditures in specific regions of Africa, Asia, and the Middle East, while lower shares are observed across most high-income regions.
